content.content:
1	# Changelog
2	
3	All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
4	
5	## Unreleased
6	
7	## [0.4.0] - 2026-06-20
8	
9	### Added
10	
11	- Add a public helper for applying sampled VMD light state to a Three.js
12	  directional light.
13	- Add a portable camera/light VMD visual smoke profile so VMD camera and light
14	  tracks are verified through rendered output.
15	
16	### Changed
17	
18	- Rework the MMD-compatible material shader path around gamma-space
19	  `diffuse * light + ambient`, MMD default light color/direction, half-lambert
20	  toon response, and view-space sphere texture coordinates.
21	- Update the viewer light-motion path to use the shared VMD light helper.
22	- Refresh generated-PMX visual baselines against the current MMD-compatible
23	  shading, alpha, and outline behavior.
24	
25	### Fixed
26	
27	- Draw inverted-hull outlines as flat texture-independent edges and keep outline
28	  width stable across hi-DPI and supersampled rendering.
29	- Preserve MMD-style alpha behavior for TGA alpha blending and texture cutout
30	  outline cases under geometry-aware alpha evaluation.
31	- Avoid per-frame allocations while syncing MMD light direction/material shader
32	  uniforms during playback.
33	
34	### Test
35	
36	- Add rendered-output visual coverage for camera VMD and light VMD application.
37	- Add generated visual baselines for edge-order and texture alpha cutout cases.
38	- Document local-only visual baseline refresh requirements for rendering
39	  changes.
40	
41	## [0.3.1] - 2026-06-16
42	
43	### Added
44	
45	- Add a bundled viewer stand-in model for motion-first local playback flows.
46	
47	### Changed
48	
49	- Update the bundled mmd-anim WASM runtime/parser artifacts to v0.1.3.
50	
51	## [0.3.0] - 2026-06-06
52	
53	### Added
54	
55	- Add the mmd-anim backed parser/runtime path and make the mmd-anim runtime the
56	  default PMX runtime path.
57	- Add experimental custom runtime support for plugging alternate model runtime
58	  implementations into the Three.js loader.
59	- Add VMD camera and light motion state sampling/application helpers.
60	- Add Three.js shadow-map based self-shadow support with VMD self-shadow
61	  sampling.
62	- Add the experimental direct-buffer MMD Bullet browser backend.
63	- Add the preferred `ThreeMmdModel.root`, `ThreeMmdModel.setAnimation(...)`,
64	  `ThreeMmdModel.update(...)`, `ThreeMmdModel.diagnostics.textures`,
65	  `ThreeMmdLoadModelOptions.outline`, and
66	  `ThreeMmdLoadModelOptions.materialRenderOrder` API surface.
67	
68	### Changed
69	
70	- Move native dependencies under `native/third_party/` and keep the MMD Bullet
71	  binding source under `native/bullet-mmd/`.
72	- Rename the MMD Bullet browser artifact and C ABI prefix to `mmd_bullet`.
73	- Consolidate native WASM build notes into `docs/DEVELOPMENT.md`.
74	- Update the Vitest release test runner to 4.1.8 and clear the npm audit gate.
75	
76	### Fixed
77	
78	- Disable the stale Yw MMD core WASM loader path in favor of the mmd-anim
79	  generated wrapper.
80	- Avoid CI source submodule checkout issues.
81	- Preserve parsed scene state across the custom runtime path.
82	
83	### Deprecated
84	
85	- Deprecate `ThreeMmdModel.object`, `ThreeMmdModel.textureDiagnostics`,
86	  `ThreeMmdLoadModelOptions.outlines`, and
87	  `ThreeMmdLoadModelOptions.renderOrderProxies`. These aliases now emit a
88	  one-time runtime warning when used and are planned for removal in the next
89	  breaking release. Root exports remain supported.
90	
91	## [0.2.2] - 2026-05-28
92	
93	### Added
94	
95	- Add VMD camera motion playback support in the viewer and Three.js runtime
96	  helper path.
97	- Add localized viewer UI copy and expanded asset/playback controls for local
98	  MMD verification workflows.
99	
100	### Changed
101	
102	- Reduce per-frame runtime allocations in animation, append transform, and IK
103	  evaluation paths.
104	- Simplify the viewer fixture/preset UI when no local fixture inventory is
105	  available, including folder-only model/background loading.
106	- Clarify the release flow from `develop` to `main` before tag-triggered npm
107	  publishing.
108	- Refresh README demo and compatibility notes for the current viewer and
109	  runtime surface.
110	
111	### Fixed
112	
113	- Honor VMD IK enable tracks and camera state when applying motion data.
114	- Place the viewer import map before module loads so browser module resolution
115	  is initialized before dependent scripts execute.
116	- Address parser/runtime review follow-ups from the post-0.2.1 changes.
117	
118	## [0.2.1] - 2026-05-26
119	
120	### Added
121	
122	- Add viewer asset presets and expanded loader controls for local playback
123	  verification.
124	- Add local Ammo playback stability coverage for dynamic bone anchoring and
125	  hierarchy behavior.
126	- Add generated visual regression fixtures for material morph alpha and hair
127	  shadow transparency cases.
128	
129	### Changed
130	
131	- Pack VMD tracks into compact runtime storage by default to reduce animation
132	  memory overhead.
133	- Improve PMX twist IK solving and default IK convergence tolerance behavior.
134	- Avoid per-frame cached ancestor allocation in the physics path.
135	- Expand viewer loading helpers for model, motion, camera, audio, and
136	  background assets.
137	
138	### Fixed
139	
140	- Guard MMD texture alpha transparency so stale morph alpha state does not leak
141	  into material transparency.
142	- Align dynamic-with-bone hierarchy handling in Ammo physics.
143	- Gate fixed-axis IK behavior to hand twist chains.
144	- Align dist export smoke coverage with packed VMD tracks.
145	
146	## [0.2.0] - 2026-05-25
147	
148	### Added
149	
150	- Add the nanoem-backed WASM parser core and route viewer model loading through
151	  the WASM-backed path when available.
152	- Add DDS texture loading support, including viewer `DDSLoader` integration and
153	  diagnostics for unsupported texture formats.
154	- Add PMM parser entry points and tests for PMM project metadata parsing.
155	- Add generated-PMX visual regression cases and committed baselines for outline,
156	  material ordering, and texture-alpha behavior.
157	- Add local native-nanoem playback oracle tooling for motion/runtime parity
158	  checks.
159	- Add `ThreeMmdModel.object` as the scene-ready root containing the base mesh
160	  and generated proxy meshes.
161	- Add a release checklist under `docs/RELEASE.md`.
162	
163	### Changed
164	
165	- Move WASM support under the parser module pipeline and build the generated
166	  WASM wrapper in CI/release packaging instead of committing generated binaries.
167	- Default the Three.js loader to the `mmdCompat` draw path with PMX-order
168	  two-pass outline rendering.
169	- Remove the earlier `outlineMode` and `renderOrderProxies` options in favor of
170	  the `mmdCompat` compatibility path.
171	- Improve outline screen-space width matching and runtime proxy mesh
172	  synchronization.
173	- Decouple local corpus smoke tests from the local asset database and document
174	  the local corpus/playback oracle workflows.
175	- Link the release checklist from the README and development documentation.
176	
177	### Fixed
178	
179	- Parse morph-only VMD files that omit the camera section.
180	- Treat oversized optional VMD tail counts as trailing data instead of failing
181	  the parse.
182	- Harden WASM parser loading and texture handling review issues.
183	- Keep third-party submodules out of the lint target and generate minimal PMX
184	  fixtures for WASM metadata tests when needed.
185	
186	## [0.1.2] - 2026-05-20
187	
188	### Changed
189	
190	- Relax the Three.js peer dependency range to allow newer 0.x releases.
191	- Verify the development toolchain against Three.js 0.184.x.
192	
193	## [0.1.1] - 2026-05-20
194	
195	### Changed
196	
197	- Harden the GitHub Actions release workflow for npm trusted publishing.
198	- Add fixture parsing to the release gate before packaging.
199	- Use the HTTPS GitHub repository URL in package metadata.
200	
201	## [0.1.0] - 2026-05-19
202	
203	Initial public release candidate for `@yohawing/three-mmd-loader`.
204	
205	### Added
206	
207	- PMX and PMD model parsing and Three.js `SkinnedMesh` assembly.
208	- VMD motion parsing and runtime application for bone and morph animation.
209	- VPD pose parsing and pose-to-animation loading.
210	- `ThreeMmdLoader` facade for model, animation, and pose loading.
211	- Default MMD runtime with frame evaluation, ticking, append transforms, and CCD IK.
212	- PMX layer-order append transform evaluation.
213	- VMD Bezier interpolation for animation sampling.
214	- Material, toon texture, sphere texture, outline, and render-order helpers.
215	- Texture diagnostics for recoverable texture resolution failures.
216	- Disabled physics backend and isolated Ammo.js physics backend boundary.
217	- Public package entry points:
218	  - `@yohawing/three-mmd-loader`
219	  - `@yohawing/three-mmd-loader/parser`
220	  - `@yohawing/three-mmd-loader/runtime`
221	  - `@yohawing/three-mmd-loader/three`
222	  - `@yohawing/three-mmd-loader/physics`
223	- Dist export smoke tests and fixture-based release evidence.
224	- Local visual regression scripts for material cases and user-owned real-model checks.
225	
226	### Known Limitations
227	
228	- Camera motion is parsed but not applied as a Three.js camera runtime feature.
229	- IK link-local / parent-local clamps support the fixed single-axis case; multi-axis behavior is partial.
230	- Ammo.js behavior is available through an explicit backend boundary and is not claimed to be native-MMD equivalent.
231	- Real-model visual regression assets are local-only and are not bundled with the package.
232	- PMM project files and `.x` / `.vac` accessories are out of scope for the initial release.
233

1	# Release
2	
3	This checklist is the operator-facing release procedure for
4	`@yohawing/three-mmd-loader`. Development details for individual scripts live in
5	[DEVELOPMENT.md](./DEVELOPMENT.md).
6	
7	## 1. Preflight
8	
9	- Confirm the target version and release scope.
10	- Confirm `CHANGELOG.md` has an entry for the target version.
11	- Build the `CHANGELOG.md` entry from the commits since the previous release
12	  tag, not only from the final release-prep commit.
13	- Confirm `package.json` `version` matches the intended release.
14	- Confirm the working tree is clean.
15	- Confirm the release branch is based on `develop`.
16	- Confirm npm Trusted Publishing is configured for the GitHub `npm`
17	  environment.
18	
19	```bash
20	git status --short
21	npm ci
22	```
23	
24	## 2. Local Checks
25	
26	Run the same release-blocking checks that CI runs before packaging:
27	
28	```bash
29	npm run lint
30	npm test
31	npm run build
32	npm run check:fixtures
33	npm run smoke:dist
34	npm run smoke:types
35	npm pack --dry-run --json
36	```
37	
38	If the `native/third_party/mmd-anim` submodule or the WASM export surface changed,
39	rebuild the WASM wrapper before the normal build:
40	
41	```bash
42	npm run build:mmd-anim
43	npm run build
44	```
45	
46	If this release changes rendering (material/outline/shader) or the mmd-anim
47	parser/runtime/WASM artifacts, also refresh and review the generated-PMX visual
48	baselines before tagging. They are **not** CI-gated (GPU/platform-specific
49	output, see [DEVELOPMENT.md](./DEVELOPMENT.md) → Visual Regression Scripts) and
50	can otherwise ship stale:
51	
52	```bash
53	npm run visual:smoke:generated-pmx
54	npm run visual:smoke:camera-light-vmd
55	npm run visual:smoke:self-shadow
56	# if rendering legitimately changed: regenerate, review the PNG diffs, re-run
57	# the smoke until green, then commit the reviewed baselines
58	npm run render:visual:generated-pmx:baseline
59	npm run render:visual:camera-light-vmd:baseline
60	```
61	
62	## 3. Commit
63	
64	Commit the version, changelog, and any release-prep changes before tagging.
65	
66	```bash
67	git status --short
68	git add package.json package-lock.json CHANGELOG.md
69	git commit -m "chore(release): vX.Y.Z"
70	```
71	
72	Adjust the staged files if the release includes additional documentation or
73	source changes.
74	
75	Push the release-prep commit to `develop` before opening the release PR.
76	
77	```bash
78	git push origin develop
79	```
80	
81	## 4. Merge `develop` to `main`
82	
83	Open a pull request from `develop` to `main` before tagging. Do not publish from
84	`develop`; the release tag must be created from the reviewed and merged `main`
85	commit.
86	
87	```bash
88	gh pr create --base main --head develop --title "chore(release): vX.Y.Z"
89	```
90	
91	Review the PR, wait for CI, and merge it into `main`. Direct pushes to `main`
92	may be blocked by repository rules; use the PR path even when the merge is a
93	fast-forward.
94	
95	After merging, update the local `main` branch and confirm that `package.json`
96	still matches the intended version.
97	
98	```bash
99	git fetch origin
100	git switch main
101	git pull --ff-only origin main
102	node -e "const p=require('./package.json'); if (p.version !== 'X.Y.Z') process.exit(1)"
103	```
104	
105	## 5. Tag
106	
107	The release workflow requires the Git tag to match `package.json` exactly.
108	Create the tag on `main` after the release PR has been reviewed and merged.
109	
110	```bash
111	git tag vX.Y.Z
112	git push origin vX.Y.Z
113	```
114	
115	For example, `package.json` version `0.2.0` must be tagged as `v0.2.0`.
116	
117	## 6. GitHub Actions
118	
119	The `Release` workflow runs on `v*.*.*` tags. It builds the package, validates
120	tag/version consistency, creates the npm tarball, publishes to npm, and creates
121	or updates the GitHub Release.
122	
123	Verify these workflow results:
124	
125	- The package job passed `lint`, `test`, `build`, fixture checks, dist smoke,
126	  and TypeScript consumer smoke.
127	- The publish job ran under the GitHub `npm` environment.
128	- npm Trusted Publishing succeeded.
129	- The GitHub Release exists for the tag, has the package artifact attached, and
130	  uses the matching `CHANGELOG.md` version section as its release notes.
131	
132	The release workflow extracts the `## [X.Y.Z]` section from `CHANGELOG.md` for
133	tag `vX.Y.Z`. If that section is missing, the workflow fails instead of
134	publishing generic generated notes.
135	
136	Manual dispatch is also available, but use tag-triggered releases for normal
137	publishing so the GitHub Release is tied to the version tag.
138	
139	## 7. Post-release Verification
140	
141	Verify the published npm artifact rather than only the local package:
142	
143	```bash
144	npm view @yohawing/three-mmd-loader version
145	npm view @yohawing/three-mmd-loader peerDependencies
146	npm view @yohawing/three-mmd-loader dist-tags
147	```
148	
149	When compatibility matters for a downstream app, install the published version
150	there or in a temporary consumer before updating that app.
151

I've successfully read all three files. Let me analyze the findings:

## Investigation Report

### 1. **src/three/index.ts - Exports Check**

**Status: PROPERLY EXPORTED**

- Line 48: `export { applyMmdCameraStateToThreeCamera } from "./camera.js";`
- Line 48: `export { applyMmdLightStateToThreeDirectionalLight } from "./light.js";`
- Type exports on lines 120-122:
  - `ApplyMmdCameraStateOptions` from "./camera.js"
  - `ApplyMmdLightStateOptions` from "./light.js"

Both the function and type exports for camera/light state are present and properly exported. No issues detected here.

---

### 2. **scripts/fixtures/generate-minimal-vmd.mjs - VMD Binary Generation Analysis**

**ISSUE FOUND: Incorrect Camera Keyframe Binary Layout**

The code has a structural mismatch with the documented VMD camera frame format:

**Lines 108-116 (Camera Frame Generation):**
```javascript
for (const frame of cameraFrames) {
    u32(frame.frame ?? 0);
    f32(frame.distance);
    f32(frame.position[0]); f32(frame.position[1]); f32(frame.position[2]);
    f32(frame.rotation[0]); f32(frame.rotation[1]); f32(frame.rotation[2]);
    out.push(...LINEAR_CAMERA_INTERPOLATION);
    u32(frame.fov);
    out.push(frame.perspective === false ? 1 : 0);
}
```

**Problems:**
1. **Byte offset mismatch**: The comment at lines 4-5 states camera frames should have 87 bytes each. Let me calculate what the code generates:
   - frame number: 4 bytes (u32)
   - distance: 4 bytes (f32)
   - position: 12 bytes (3 × f32)
   - rotation: 12 bytes (3 × f32)
   - interpolation (LINEAR_CAMERA_INTERPOLATION): 12 bytes (lines 37-42)
   - fov: 4 bytes (u32) **← Wrong type for floating-point FOV**
   - perspective: 1 byte (u8)
   - **Total: 4+4+12+12+12+4+1 = 49 bytes** (but should use f32 for FOV, making it 53 bytes minimum)

2. **FOV Type Issue (Line 114)**: `u32(frame.fov)` writes an unsigned 32-bit integer, but FOV should be a 32-bit float (f32). This corrupts the binary format.

3. **Perspective Flag Placement**: Line 115 pushes a single byte for perspective, but standard VMD format typically pads to 4-byte boundaries.

**Light Frames (Lines 118-123):** 
```javascript
for (const frame of lightFrames) {
    u32(frame.frame ?? 0);
    f32(frame.color[0]); f32(frame.color[1]); f32(frame.color[2]);
    f32(frame.direction[0]); f32(frame.direction[1]); f32(frame.direction[2]);
}
```

Light frame generation looks structurally correct: 4 + 12 + 12 = 28 bytes per frame (frame + RGB color + XYZ direction).

---

### 3. **test/unit/three/Materials.test.ts - New Tests**

**Status: NO NEW TESTS DETECTED**

The file contains comprehensive existing tests for MMD materials but does **not appear to have any new test additions** related to camera/light VMD functionality. The test suite covers:
- Texture loading and material creation (extensive)
- Material transparency and alpha handling
- Shader compilation and lighting
- DDS texture support

**No tests for:**
- `applyMmdLightStateToThreeDirectionalLight`
- `applyMmdCameraStateToThreeCamera`
- VMD camera/light frame parsing or application

---

## Summary

| Area | File | Status | Details |
|------|------|--------|---------|
| Exports | `src/three/index.ts` (lines 48, 120-122) | ✓ OK | Both functions and types properly exported |
| Camera/Light VMD Generation | `scripts/fixtures/generate-minimal-vmd.mjs` (lines 108-116) | ⚠ BUG | FOV written as u32 instead of f32; byte structure incorrect |
| Light VMD Generation | `scripts/fixtures/generate-minimal-vmd.mjs` (lines 118-123) | ✓ OK | Structurally sound |
| Test Coverage | `test/unit/three/Materials.test.ts` | ✗ MISSING | No camera/light VMD tests found in file |

**Critical Issue**: Line 114 in generate-minimal-vmd.mjs should use `f32(frame.fov)` instead of `u32(frame.fov)` to correctly generate the camera frame binary format.

3. **Perspective Flag Placement**: Line 115 pushes a single byte for perspective, but standard VMD format typically pads to 4-byte boundaries.

```javascript
for (const frame of lightFrames) {
    u32(frame.frame ?? 0);
    f32(frame.color[0]); f32(frame.color[1]); f32(frame.color[2]);
    f32(frame.direction[0]); f32(frame.direction[1]); f32(frame.direction[2]);
}
```

Light frame generation looks structurally correct: 4 + 12 + 12 = 28 bytes per frame (frame + RGB color + XYZ direction).

summary: Deep dive VMD binary format and material-sync hot path

content.text:
Perfect. I've now read both files completely and checked the git status. Let me provide you with a comprehensive analysis:

## **1. generate-minimal-vmd.mjs Analysis**

### LINEAR_CAMERA_INTERPOLATION (lines 37-42)
The `LINEAR_CAMERA_INTERPOLATION` array contains **24 bytes total**:
- 6 rows × 4 values = 24 uint8 values
- Layout: 
  - Rows 1-2 (lines 38-39): 12 bytes of `20` values (x1, x2 for 6 axes)
  - Rows 3-4 (lines 40-41): 12 bytes of `107` values (y1, y2 for 6 axes)

This represents interpolation control points for camera frames (position X/Y/Z, rotation X/Y/Z = 6 axes).

### u32(frame.fov) (line 114)
**You are correct: `u32(frame.fov)` is CORRECT.** The VMD spec stores camera FOV as uint32 (viewing angle in degrees), NOT float. The code correctly uses `u32()` here. Looking at the camera test data (lines 199-224), all fov values are integers like 24, which confirms this is the right encoding.

### Total byte count per camera frame
Calculating from the code (lines 109-116):
- frame: 4 bytes (u32)
- distance: 4 bytes (f32)
- position: 12 bytes (3 × f32)
- rotation: 12 bytes (3 × f32)
- interpolation: 24 bytes (LINEAR_CAMERA_INTERPOLATION)
- fov: 4 bytes (u32)
- perspective: 1 byte

**Total: 4 + 4 + 12 + 12 + 24 + 4 + 1 = 61 bytes per camera frame** ✓

This matches the standard VMD camera frame size you cited.

### Size validation
There are **no explicit size assertions** in this file, but the code is deterministic and the byte calculations are correct based on the encoding functions used.

---

## **2. material-sync.ts Analysis**

### syncMmdMaterialStates() entry point (lines 12-128)
This function is called with:
- `materials`: Material instance(s)
- `states`: MaterialRuntimeState array

**Where is it called from?** This requires searching the codebase, but based on the function signature, it takes a snapshot of states and synchronizes them to materials. The code uses `.forEach()` to iterate materials (line 17), and all the state copying happens within this loop.

### Lines 42-50 array spreads analysis
These lines ARE within the per-frame hot path:

```typescript
// Lines 41-51
material.userData.mmdMaterialState = {
  diffuse: [...state.diffuse],
  specular: [...state.specular],
  specularPower: state.specularPower,
  ambient: [...state.ambient],
  edgeColor: [...state.edgeColor],
  edgeSize: state.edgeSize,
  textureFactor: [...state.textureFactor],
  sphereTextureFactor: [...state.sphereTextureFactor],
  toonTextureFactor: [...state.toonTextureFactor]
};
```

These spreads **ARE inside the forEach loop** (line 17) that iterates all materials, so they execute once per material per `syncMmdMaterialStates()` call. The question is: **how often is `syncMmdMaterialStates()` called?**

### mmdMaterialState caching
Lines 41-51 **always overwrite** `material.userData.mmdMaterialState` with a new object containing spread copies of arrays. This happens on every call to `syncMmdMaterialStates()`. The object is NOT cached/reused; it's recreated each time.

**Performance observation:** Each call creates new array copies (spreads). If this is called every frame with material morphs active, this could be a hotspot. The spreads are necessary to avoid external mutations, but the frequency of calls determines the actual cost.

---

## **3. Git Status - Untracked files**

Yes, `test/fixtures/visual-baselines/camera-light-vmd/` **shows as untracked** (`??`):

```
?? test/fixtures/visual-baselines/camera-light-vmd/
```

Also new untracked files:
- `.ai/`
- `scripts/visual-regression/camera-light-vmd.manifest.json`
- `src/three/light.ts`
- `test/unit/three/Light.test.ts`

These appear to be part of a camera/light feature addition (camera-light-vmd directory, light.ts module, Light.test.ts).

1	name: Release
2	
3	on:
4	  push:
5	    tags:
6	      - "v*.*.*"
7	  workflow_dispatch:
8	    inputs:
9	      publish:
10	        description: "Publish to npm and create/update the GitHub Release"
11	        required: true
12	        type: boolean
13	        default: false
14	
15	permissions:
16	  contents: read
17	
18	concurrency:
19	  group: release-${{ github.ref }}
20	  cancel-in-progress: false
21	
22	jobs:
23	  package:
24	    name: Build release package
25	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
26	
27	    steps:
28	      - name: Checkout
29	        uses: actions/checkout@v6
30	
31	      - name: Setup Node.js
32	        uses: actions/setup-node@v6
33	        with:
34	          node-version: 24
35	          package-manager-cache: false
36	
37	      - name: Install dependencies
38	        run: npm ci
39	
40	      - name: Lint
41	        run: npm run lint
42	
43	      - name: Test
44	        run: npm test
45	
46	      - name: Build
47	        run: npm run build
48	
49	      - name: Check fixtures
50	        run: npm run check:fixtures
51	
52	      - name: Smoke dist exports
53	        run: npm run smoke:dist
54	
55	      - name: Smoke TypeScript consumer
56	        run: npm run smoke:types
57	
58	      - name: Check package publishability
59	        if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/') || inputs.publish
60	        run: |
61	          node --input-type=module <<'NODE'
62	          import fs from 'node:fs';
63	
64	          const pkg = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('package.json', 'utf8'));
65	          if (pkg.private === true) {
66	            throw new Error('package.json still has "private": true. Remove it before publishing.');
67	          }
68	          if (!pkg.name || !pkg.version) {
69	            throw new Error('package.json must include name and version before publishing.');
70	          }
71	          NODE
72	
73	      - name: Check tag matches package version
74	        if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/')
75	        run: |
76	          node --input-type=module <<'NODE'
77	          import fs from 'node:fs';
78	
79	          const pkg = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('package.json', 'utf8'));
80	          const tag = process.env.GITHUB_REF_NAME;
81	          if (tag !== `v${pkg.version}`) {
82	            throw new Error(`Tag ${tag} does not match package version v${pkg.version}.`);
83	          }
84	          NODE
85	
86	      - name: Create npm package
87	        run: npm pack --json > npm-pack.json
88	
89	      - name: Upload package artifact
90	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
91	        with:
92	          name: npm-package
93	          path: |
94	            *.tgz
95	            npm-pack.json
96	          if-no-files-found: error
97	
98	  publish:
99	    name: Publish release
100	    needs: package
101	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
102	    if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/') || inputs.publish
103	
104	    permissions:
105	      contents: write
106	      id-token: write
107	
108	    environment:
109	      name: npm
110	
111	    steps:
112	      - name: Checkout
113	        uses: actions/checkout@v6
114	
115	      - name: Setup Node.js
116	        uses: actions/setup-node@v6
117	        with:
118	          node-version: 24
119	          registry-url: https://registry.npmjs.org
120	          package-manager-cache: false
121	
122	      - name: Download package artifact
123	        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
124	        with:
125	          name: npm-package
126	
127	      - name: Publish to npm
128	        run: npm publish ./*.tgz --access public
129	
130	      - name: Extract release notes from CHANGELOG
131	        if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/')
132	        run: |
133	          node --input-type=module <<'NODE'
134	          import fs from 'node:fs';
135	
136	          const pkg = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('package.json', 'utf8'));
137	          const changelog = fs.readFileSync('CHANGELOG.md', 'utf8');
138	          const lines = changelog.split(/\r?\n/);
139	          const heading = `## [${pkg.version}]`;
140	          const start = lines.findIndex((line) => line.startsWith(heading));
141	          if (start === -1) {
142	            throw new Error(`CHANGELOG.md does not contain a ## [${pkg.version}] section.`);
143	          }
144	          const endOffset = lines
145	            .slice(start + 1)
146	            .findIndex((line) => line.startsWith('## ['));
147	          const end = endOffset === -1 ? lines.length : start + 1 + endOffset;
148	          const notes = lines.slice(start + 1, end).join('\n').trim();
149	          if (!notes) {
150	            throw new Error(`CHANGELOG.md section ## [${pkg.version}] is empty.`);
151	          }
152	
153	          fs.writeFileSync('release-notes.md', `${notes}\n`);
154	          NODE
155	
156	      - name: Create or update GitHub Release
157	        if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/')
158	        env:
159	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
160	        run: |
161	          tag="${GITHUB_REF_NAME}"
162	          package_file="$(ls ./*.tgz | head -n 1)"
163	
164	          if gh release view "$tag" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
165	            gh release edit "$tag" --notes-file release-notes.md
166	            gh release upload "$tag" "$package_file" --clobber
167	          else
168	            gh release create "$tag" "$package_file" --notes-file release-notes.md --verify-tag
169	          fi

# Review: three-mmd-loader v0.4.0 Release Diff

## Context
Codex implemented the camera/light/self-shadow VMD rendered-output release gate,
version/changelog/roadmap updates, and visual baseline refreshes for v0.4.0.
This plan records bounded-reviewer findings ordered by severity.

---

## Findings

### [BLOCKER] Untracked new files — PR would ship without them

`git status --porcelain` shows five `??` entries that are NOT staged:

| File | Why it matters |
|------|---------------|
| `src/three/light.ts` | New public module; `src/three/index.ts` imports `./light.js` |
| `test/unit/three/Light.test.ts` | Unit tests for light module |
| `scripts/visual-regression/camera-light-vmd.manifest.json` | Smoke-test manifest; `compute-metrics.mjs` references it |
| `test/fixtures/visual-baselines/camera-light-vmd/camera-near.png` | Smoke baseline |
| `test/fixtures/visual-baselines/camera-light-vmd/camera-far.png` | Smoke baseline |
| `test/fixtures/visual-baselines/camera-light-vmd/light-front.png` | Smoke baseline |
| `test/fixtures/visual-baselines/camera-light-vmd/light-side.png` | Smoke baseline |

All Codex commands passed because the files exist on disk. A PR created from
the current index would be missing these files; a fresh checkout would fail
to build and all camera-light-vmd smoke tests would fail.

**Fix**: `git add src/three/light.ts test/unit/three/Light.test.ts scripts/visual-regression/camera-light-vmd.manifest.json test/fixtures/visual-baselines/camera-light-vmd/` before committing.

Note: `test/fixtures/generated/camera-light-vmd/*.vmd` are correctly gitignored
(`.gitignore` pattern `test/fixtures/generated/**/*.vmd`). The generation script
must be run as a setup step; `npm run check:fixtures` covers this locally.

---

### [MEDIUM] material-sync.ts — per-frame spread allocations need diff verification

`syncMmdMaterialStates()` (`src/three/material/material-sync.ts`) is called
**every frame** from `src/three/runtime-sync.ts:134`.

Lines ~42–50 of the current file create 8 array spread copies + 1 new object
per material per call:

```typescript
material.userData.mmdMaterialState = {
  diffuse: [...state.diffuse],
  specular: [...state.specular],
  ambient: [...state.ambient],
  edgeColor: [...state.edgeColor],
  textureFactor: [...state.textureFactor],
  sphereTextureFactor: [...state.sphereTextureFactor],
  toonTextureFactor: [...state.toonTextureFactor],
  ...
};
```

The CHANGELOG claim "Avoid per-frame allocations while syncing MMD light
direction/material shader uniforms" demonstrably covers `syncMmdSpecularDirection`
(scratch Vector3s at module level, confirmed correct). Whether the spreads in
`syncMmdMaterialStates` are **new additions** in this diff or pre-existing is
the open question.

**Verification**: Run `git diff HEAD -- src/three/material/material-sync.ts` and
check whether lines 42–50 appear in the `+` side. If they are newly added here,
this is a regression contradicting the CHANGELOG; if pre-existing, it is a
known issue out of scope for this PR.

---

### [LOW] Self-shadow ROI narrowed aggressively

`scripts/visual-regression/self-shadow.manifest.json`:
`body-self-shadow-black-toon-does-not-collapse-to-black` ROI changed:
- Before: `{ x:124, y:322, width:210, height:20 }` (4200 px²)
- After:  `{ x:160, y:320, width:80,  height:16 }` (1280 px², ~30% of original)

A 3× area reduction increases brittleness: minor shadow placement shift or
sub-pixel rounding can move the shadow outside the ROI and silently pass the
test. Low risk for this release, but worth a comment in the PR.

---

## Non-issues (initial concerns ruled out)

| Concern | Verdict |
|---------|---------|
| `u32(frame.fov)` in `generate-minimal-vmd.mjs:114` | **Correct** — VMD spec stores camera viewing angle as uint32, not float |
| `applyMmdLightStateToThreeDirectionalLight` export | **Present** — `src/three/index.ts:48`, type also exported at lines 120-122 |
| Visual smoke profile not proving camera/light VMD application | **Disproved** — `render-real-models.mjs` lines 441-481 actively call `sampleMmdLightTrack`/`applyMmdLightStateToThreeDirectionalLight` and `sampleMmdCameraTrack`/`applyMmdCameraStateToThreeCamera` |
| Changelog/package/roadmap inconsistency | **None found** — `package.json` version is `0.4.0`, CHANGELOG v0.4.0 section is complete |

---

## Residual risks

- **CI does not run visual smoke tests** (pre-existing, by design). The gate
  is manual-only. Document this in the PR description so reviewers know.
- **Generated VMD fixtures are gitignored** — any CI job wanting to run
  `visual:smoke:camera-light-vmd` must run `npm run check:fixtures` first.
  The existing CI workflow does not, but since visual smoke is not CI-gated,
  this is low risk for now.

---

## Verification (for implementer)

1. `git add` the five untracked paths listed above.
2. `git diff --cached --name-only` — confirm all seven new files appear.
3. `git diff HEAD -- src/three/material/material-sync.ts` — confirm whether lines
   42–50 spreads are `+` (new) or unchanged context.
4. Re-run `npm test` and `npm run build` on the staged state to confirm nothing
   regressed after the adds.
